action_items.description: Replace AUROC=0.206 with AUROC=0.350 (Table 1 raw cosine) in Introduction (line 59), Contribution 2 block, and Conclusion (line 993). Add footnote clarifying 0.350 is the intra-feature raw cosine (1-EDA) not the inter-feature decoder-decoder cosine.

Replace '0.919-0.968' and '92-97%' with '0.876-0.978' in Introduction, Contribution 3 block, and Conclusion. Add a single clarifying sentence: 'Within the primary jb suite (layers 2-10), rates span 0.938-0.967; including AJT configurations, the full range is 0.876-0.978.'

Verify E1_phase_transition.json canonical values. Use a single Spearman rho for the phase stability claim throughout the paper (both Introduction and Section 4.4). Verify whether rho=0.191 (from B2 EDA_delta vs 1/L0) and rho=-0.482 (from E1 absorption_rate vs 1/L0) are measuring different variables — if so, remove the E1 rho from the Introduction and keep only the EDA_delta rho there.
action_items.requires: Cross-reference exp/results/full/B2_scaling_curve.json and exp/results/full/E1_phase_transition.json to identify which rho corresponds to which analysis.

action_items.description: Add the abstract from writing/outline.md before the Introduction. Verify all numerical claims in the abstract against the corrected values from items 1-3. In particular, update absorption range ('0.919-0.968' → '0.876-0.978') and verify AUROC citations.

action_items.action: Fix roadmap sentence and section numbering
action_items.description: Rewrite the paper structure roadmap in Introduction (line 77) to correctly describe the linear section order. Standardize section numbering: remove inline '# 4 Experiments' numeric prefix; ensure LaTeX will auto-number Introduction, Related Work, Method, Experiments, Discussion, Conclusion as sections 1-6.
Remove the image embed at line 82 (between Introduction and Related Work). Replace with a forward reference: 'Figure~\ref{fig:method} in Section~\ref{sec:method} illustrates this geometry.'

action_items.action: Report cos(ê_p, d_c) against exact labels
action_items.description: The cross-directional metric cos(ê_p, d_c) is reported at AUROC=0.730 against proxy labels (n_pos=50) but not against exact labels (n_pos=18). Either compute this metric against exact labels and add to Table 1, or add a clear note explaining the metric is only validated against proxy labels and the Jaccard overlap=0.115 means the comparison to EDA (exact labels) is not direct.

action_items.action: Add explicit Table 1 separator for exact vs. proxy labels
action_items.description: Add a visible horizontal rule or dagger notation in Table 1 to separate EDA (exact labels, n_pos=18) from cross-directional metrics (proxy labels, n_pos=50). The footnote should include the Jaccard overlap=0.115 to make explicit that these are largely non-overlapping label sets.

action_items.action: Revise semantic hierarchy null result framing
action_items.description: Section 4.5 and 5.5 currently claim 'feature absorption in GPT-2 Small is specific to orthographic hierarchies.' This is overstated given n=20 words per semantic hierarchy. Revise to: 'absorption was not detectable in semantic hierarchies with n=20 words per hierarchy using the top-5 parent identification method; this null result is underpowered and does not rule out semantic absorption.'

Remove 'consistent with the absorbed state being metastable' from the Conclusion. Replace with: 'all tested configurations show absorption rates near 0.96 regardless of training path, indicating that within the tested L0 range, no non-absorbed stable state is accessible via sparsity reduction; classical hysteresis testing requires a non-absorbed equilibrium which does not exist in this regime.'

Add a sentence in Section 3.3 or 4.1 explicitly stating: 'Proposition 1 predicts that absorbed pairs have smaller decoder-decoder angles (higher cos^2(theta)); empirically, the opposite is found (absorbed pairs have lower cos^2(theta), AUROC=0.318, Cohen's d=-0.57). This indicates that Proposition 1's threshold describes absorption onset geometry, not the post-convergence equilibrium state.'

Add an explicit caveat in Section 4.3 for the B3 TopK comparison: Standard uses resid_pre, TopK uses resid_post at layer 6. The EDA comparison between the two architectures is confounded by both this hook-point difference and the L0 mismatch (32 vs. 51). Reframe the TopK comparison as indicative only.

action_items.action: Add sae-spelling commit hash and FeatureAbsorptionCalculator parameters to Methods
action_items.description: Section 3.4 must specify: (1) sae-spelling repository commit hash used, (2) FeatureAbsorptionCalculator parameters (threshold, n_tokens, letter selection criterion), (3) SAELens release hash for gpt2-small-res-jb, (4) the 43 independent letter feature IDs used as parent candidates in D2.

action_items.action: Remove duplicate EDA magnitude tension discussion
action_items.description: Remove the brief 'We report this as an open question' mention from Section 4.2. Retain only the fuller treatment in Section 5.2 with the temporal-drift reconciliation hypothesis.

action_items.action: Verify animate_inanimate F1=1.0 with shuffled control
action_items.description: The animate_inanimate probe achieves F1=1.0 on n_test=22, flagged as suspicious in audit. Verify that the shuffled-label F1 is near random (not also near 1.0) before reporting this hierarchy as 'passing the F1 gate.' Report n_train, n_test, and shuffled-F1 in the paper or supplement.
